title: Process dictionary — put/get/erase BIFs
purpose: Every BEAM process has a mutable key-value dictionary, local and private to that process. It's the one piece of mutable state in the process model that breaks referential transparency — which is precisely why it exists: pragmatic escape hatch for per-process caches, counters, and metadata that would otherwise require threading state through every function call. The process dictionary is accessed via BIFs: put/2, get/1, get/0, erase/1, erase/0, get_keys/1. OTP libraries use it extensively — gen_server stores $gen_call tags, logger stores process metadata, random stores seed state. Without it, these modules fail. The critical design concern is GC integration: the dictionary contains Terms that may point to heap-allocated objects (tuples, lists, closures, etc.). The GC must trace these Terms as roots and rewrite them when objects are relocated during collection. The existing root tracing pattern (x_regs → y_regs → mailbox → exception) must extend to include dictionary entries.
task: Add a Vec<(Term, Term)> dictionary field to Process with GC root integration, implement 6 dictionary accessor methods on Process, and register 6 process dictionary BIFs (put/2, get/1, get/0, erase/1, erase/0, get_keys/1) in a new dictionary_bifs.rs module.

requirements.title: Dictionary field on Process with GC root integration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a dictionary: Vec<(Term, Term)> field to Process, initialized empty in Process::new(). WHEN roots_with_live_x() collects GC roots, THE SYSTEM SHALL chain dictionary entries into the root collection after exception roots, iterating pairs in Vec order with key then value for each pair. WHEN replace_roots_with_live_x() writes back roots, THE SYSTEM SHALL rewrite both keys and values in place when GC relocates heap objects.
acceptance: Process gains field dictionary: Vec<(Term, Term)> — NOT HashMap. HashMap<Term, Term> is broken for boxed keys: Term's Hash impl uses the raw u64 (pointer value). After GC relocates a boxed term, its pointer changes. The entry stays in the old hash bucket, but lookups with the new pointer hash to a different bucket — silent miss. GC root rewriting updates the Term value but cannot re-bucket the HashMap entry. Vec with linear scan avoids this entirely. Process dictionaries are typically small (<20 entries); O(n) lookup is fine.
Initialized empty in Process::new()

roots_with_live_x() chains dictionary entries into the root collection after exception roots. Order: pairs in Vec order, key then value for each pair. This order is stable across GC cycles since Vec preserves insertion order.

replace_roots_with_live_x() writes back dictionary roots in the same order. Both keys and values are rewritten in place when GC relocates heap objects. Since we use Vec (not HashMap), the rewritten key values don't break any bucketing.
Test: allocate a tuple on the heap, store it in the dictionary via put. Trigger minor GC. Verify the dictionary entry still points to a valid tuple (the reference was rewritten by GC).
Test: dictionary entries with non-heap terms (small integers, atoms) survive GC unchanged.

requirements.title: Dictionary accessor methods on Process
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose dictionary operations as Process methods: dict_put (linear scan using compare::exact_eq, insert or update, returns old value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)), dict_get (linear scan, returns value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)), dict_get_all (borrow all entries), dict_erase (linear scan, remove via swap_remove, returns old value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)), dict_erase_all (drain and return all entries), and dict_get_keys (linear scan for matching value using compare::exact_eq).
acceptance: Process::dict_put(&mut self, key: Term, value: Term) -> Term — linear scan for matching key (using compare::exact_eq), insert or update, returns old value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)
Process::dict_get(&self, key: Term) -> Term — linear scan for matching key (using compare::exact_eq), returns value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)
Process::dict_get_all(&self) -> &[(Term, Term)] — borrow all entries
Process::dict_erase(&mut self, key: Term) -> Term — linear scan, remove via swap_remove, returns old value or Term::atom(Atom::UNDEFINED)
Process::dict_erase_all(&mut self) -> Vec<(Term, Term)> — drain and return all entries
Process::dict_get_keys(&self, value: Term) -> Vec<Term> — linear scan for matching value (uses compare::exact_eq, per BEAM semantics)
Test: put/get round-trip
Test: put replaces existing entry, returns old value
Test: get on missing key returns undefined
Test: erase on missing key returns undefined

requirements.title: Process dictionary BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL register 6 process dictionary BIFs, all with Capability::ProcessLocal: erlang:put/2 (calls process.dict_put, returns old value), erlang:get/1 (calls process.dict_get, returns value or undefined), erlang:get/0 (calls process.dict_get_all, builds list of {Key, Value} 2-tuples on heap), erlang:erase/1 (calls process.dict_erase, returns old value), erlang:erase/0 (calls process.dict_erase_all, builds list of {Key, Value} 2-tuples on heap), and erlang:get_keys/1 (calls process.dict_get_keys, builds list of keys on heap). WHEN the heap is full during list construction for get/0, erase/0, or get_keys/1, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ExecError::HeapFull.
acceptance: erlang:put/2 — calls process.dict_put(key, value), returns old value
erlang:get/1 — calls process.dict_get(key), returns value or undefined
erlang:get/0 — calls process.dict_get_all(), builds list of {Key, Value} 2-tuples on heap, returns list
erlang:erase/1 — calls process.dict_erase(key), returns old value
erlang:erase/0 — calls process.dict_erase_all(), builds list of {Key, Value} 2-tuples on heap, returns list
erlang:get_keys/1 — calls process.dict_get_keys(value), builds list of keys on heap, returns list
For get/0, erase/0, and get_keys/1: heap allocation for the result list. If heap is full, return ExecError::HeapFull.
Test: from BIF mechanism, put a value, get it back, erase it, verify get returns undefined
Test: get/0 returns complete dictionary as list of tuples

boundaries: SHALL add a Vec<(Term, Term)> field to Process (NOT HashMap — see R1 rationale)
SHALL integrate dictionary terms into the GC root set
SHALL NOT add any locking — the dictionary is process-local, no concurrency
SHALL NOT implement process_info(Pid, dictionary) — that's a separate introspection concern
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new dictionary tests
GC correctly traces and rewrites dictionary entries containing heap-allocated terms
All 6 BIFs registered and callable through the BIF mechanism
Dictionary operations use Vec with linear scan, not HashMap
put/get/erase round-trip works correctly through BIF interface
